title: Fix T1000-E puck LR1110 loramac-node build source path
description: Discovered during project-LICHEN-zte2.3 no-hardware validation. Building lichen/apps/puck for t1000_e/nrf52840 fails during CMake generation before application code links because lichen/drivers/lora/lr1110/CMakeLists.txt references missing loramac-node source modules/lib/loramac-node/src/radio/lr1110/lr1110_driver/src/lr1110_radio.c. Command: west build -p always -b t1000_e/nrf52840 lichen/apps/puck -d build-puck-t1000-location-v1 -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=$PWD/lichen. Acceptance: t1000_e/nrf52840 puck app reaches compile/link or the T1000-E LR1110 puck target is explicitly marked unsupported with a tracked replacement path; no physical hardware required for build fix, physical radio smoke remains separate.
notes: Implementation and no-hardware validation completed 2026-07-02. Local changes: tools/zephyr-clean-worktree.sh now initializes loramac-node nested submodule src/radio/lr1110/lr1110_driver when preparing a clean Zephyr worktree if lr1110_radio.c is absent; lichen/drivers/lora/lr1110/CMakeLists.txt now fails fast with an actionable message if LR1110 sources are still missing. Local checks: bash -n tools/zephyr-clean-worktree.sh PASS; git diff --check PASS. Independent codereviews: Lovelace, Franklin, and Arendt reported no P0/P1/P2 findings after inspection. EC2 validation: instance i-0e92a68a2ffd0ab23, SG sg-01f2a2446bc3d7e4d, EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58 mounted at /mnt/lichen-zephyr. bootstrap-host.sh hit Ubuntu Noble awscli package unavailability, so equivalent Zephyr host packages were installed directly; env.sh then succeeded. Validation worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-zte2-3-1-validate created from remote plus patch; patched setup printed 'Initializing loramac-node LR1110 driver submodule in cache...' and checked out lr1110_driver commit 1da8583480d03e5fbd43792a2a17db7ca2d9f772. Build command: SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=1717777777 west build -p always -b t1000_e/nrf52840 lichen/apps/puck -d build-puck-t1000-location-v1 -- -DZEPHYR_EXTRA_MODULES=/Users/markatwood/Desktop/Projects/project-LICHEN/lichen. Result: PASS through link/generate zephyr.elf; CMake moved past previous missing-source failure and compiled lr1110_radio.c, lr1110_regmem.c, lr1110_system.c, and lr1110.c. Zephyr memory report: FLASH 164484 B / 360 KB = 44.62%; RAM 51284 B / 256 KB = 19.56%. size build-puck-t1000-location-v1/zephyr/zephyr.elf: text 160444, data 4031, bss 47575, dec 212050. Verified module isolation: tools/zephyr-clean-worktree.sh verify "/Users/markatwood/Desktop/Projects/project-LICHEN" build-puck-t1000-location-v1 PASS. Config evidence: CONFIG_BOARD_T1000_E=y, CONFIG_GNSS=y, CONFIG_GNSS_AG3335=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_HAS_GNSS=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_LOCATION_PROVIDER_GNSS=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_TIME_BUILD_EPOCH_SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_TIME_BUILD_EPOCH_UNIX=1717777777, CONFIG_LICHEN_TIME_PROVIDER_GNSS=y, CONFIG_LICHEN_RADIO_MODEL_LR1110=y, CONFIG_LORA_LR1110=y. Notable warnings retained for separate work: unknown devicetree vendor prefix airoha for ag3335, duplicate unit-address warnings in nRF DTS, CMake no SOURCES for Zephyr library loramac-node and lichen__drivers__lora excluding target, MCUboot unsigned/no key warning, USB_CDC_ACM_LOG_LEVEL forced to LOG_LEVEL_NONE, and unused HAL charger helper functions in hal.c. No physical hardware used.
close_reason: Completed no-hardware LR1110 build fix. Clean Zephyr worktree setup now initializes the loramac-node LR1110 nested submodule, CMake reports an actionable missing-source error if the source is absent, and EC2 t1000_e/nrf52840 puck build passed through link with CONFIG_LORA_LR1110=y. Physical radio smoke remains separate hardware work.
